File naming: {timestamp}-{id}.json. Create src/ipc/ipc-reader.ts: DirectoryPoller class - polls directory at configurable interval (default 500ms), reads files sorted by name (timestamp order), validates with Zod, emits parsed messages, deletes processed files, moves invalid files to errors/ subdirectory. Create src/ipc/ipc-channel.ts: BidirectionalIpcChannel combining writer+reader for a thread. Create src/ipc/daemon-ipc.ts: DaemonIpc for talonctl<->talond communication (same pattern, different directory).

completion_spec.outcome: IPC round-trip works: write message to input/, reader picks it up, validates, processes, deletes. Invalid messages go to errors/. Integration test with actual filesystem.

data.title: Sandbox manager: Docker container lifecycle
data.description: Create src/sandbox/sandbox-types.ts: SandboxState enum (spawning, warm, busy, shutting_down), SandboxConfig, ContainerInfo. Create src/sandbox/container-factory.ts: createContainer(config, threadId, persona) using dockerode - creates Docker container with security hardening: --cap-drop=ALL, --read-only rootfs, tmpfs /tmp, no Docker socket, resource limits (CPU, memory, PIDs), network off by default, seccomp profile. Mount thread directories (memory/ RO, artifacts/ RW, ipc/ RW). Create src/sandbox/sandbox-manager.ts: SandboxManager class - spawnContainer, getContainer(threadId), listContainers, killContainer, handleIdleTimeout (default 30min). Tracks container state, respects maxConcurrent global limit, evicts oldest-idle when at capacity. Create src/sandbox/container-health.ts: periodic health checks on warm containers.

completion_spec.outcome: Can spawn, track, and kill Docker containers programmatically. Containers are security-hardened. Integration test: spawn container, verify mounts, verify capabilities dropped, kill container.

data.title: SDK process spawner: run Claude Code inside containers
data.description: Create src/sandbox/sdk-process-spawner.ts: spawnSdkProcess(container, config) that executes Claude Code CLI inside a running Docker container using dockerode exec. Deliver initial config (secrets, persona system prompt, session ID) via stdin JSON at spawn time. Handle stdout/stderr streaming from the exec. Parse output using sentinel markers (---TALOND_OUTPUT_START--- / ---TALOND_OUTPUT_END---). Strip internal tags before passing output back. Create src/sandbox/session-tracker.ts: SessionTracker class - track SDK session IDs per thread for session resumption across messages. Handle session resumption via resume parameter when delivering follow-up messages.

completion_spec.outcome: Can execute Claude Code CLI inside a Docker container, deliver config via stdin, and parse structured output from sentinel markers. Session IDs tracked per thread.

data.title: Tool system: registry, capability resolver, policy engine
data.description: Create src/tools/tool-types.ts: ToolManifest (name, description, capabilities required, parameter schema, execution location: host/sandbox/mcp). Create src/tools/tool-registry.ts: ToolRegistry class - register tools, lookup by name, list by capability. Create src/tools/capability-resolver.ts: resolveCapabilities(persona, skill) computes intersection of persona.allow and skill.requiredCapabilities. Validate capability labels at config load time (catch typos). Create src/tools/policy-engine.ts: PolicyEngine class - evaluateToolCall(persona, tool, args) returns Allow/Deny/RequireApproval. Implemented as SDK PreToolUse hooks. Rate limiting per tool/persona. Create src/tools/approval-gate.ts: ApprovalGate placeholder (in-channel approval prompting - full implementation in Phase 6). Create src/tools/host-tools/ directory with stubs for: channel-send.ts, schedule-manage.ts, memory-access.ts, http-proxy.ts, db-query.ts.

completion_spec.outcome: Tool registry accepts manifests, capability resolver computes intersections, policy engine evaluates allow/deny/approve. Unit tests for capability resolution and policy evaluation.

data.title: Persona system: loading, capability resolution, skill integration
data.description: Create src/personas/persona-types.ts: PersonaConfig type, ResolvedPersona (with computed capabilities). Create src/personas/capability-types.ts: CapabilityLabel type (string pattern like 'fs.read:workspace'), capability validation. Create src/personas/persona-loader.ts: PersonaLoader class - loadPersonas(config) reads persona configs, resolves capabilities against skills, validates system prompt files exist, returns ResolvedPersona[]. Logs warnings for skills with unmet capabilities (not silent failure). Handles persona reload for hot-reload support.

completion_spec.outcome: Personas load from config, capabilities resolve correctly, skill integration works. Unit tests for capability resolution including edge cases (no skills, conflicting capabilities).

data.title: Memory system: thread workspace, context builder
data.description: Create src/memory/memory-types.ts: MemoryLayer enum, MemoryItem types. Create src/memory/thread-workspace.ts: ThreadWorkspace class - ensureDirectories(threadId) creates data/threads/{id}/memory/, attachments/, artifacts/, ipc/input/, ipc/output/, ipc/errors/. Manages thread directory lifecycle. Create src/memory/memory-manager.ts: MemoryManager class - readThreadMemory (from DB + files), writeMemoryItem (policy-gated by persona capabilities), getWorkingMemory (recent message window). Create src/memory/context-builder.ts: buildContext(threadId, persona) assembles context from memory layers (transcript from DB, working memory window, thread notebook files, structured facts) for agent prompts.

completion_spec.outcome: Thread directories created correctly. Memory items read/written through manager. Context builder assembles multi-layer context. Unit tests for each layer.

data.title: Queue system: durable queue, retry/backoff, dead-letter
data.description: Create src/queue/queue-types.ts: QueueItemStatus enum (pending, claimed, processing, completed, failed, dead_letter), QueueItem type. Create src/queue/retry-strategy.ts: calculateBackoff(attempt, baseMs, maxMs) with exponential backoff + jitter. Create src/queue/dead-letter.ts: DeadLetterHandler - move items to dead_letter status with human-readable reason. Create src/queue/queue-processor.ts: QueueProcessor class - dequeue next item for a thread (FIFO), claim with DB update, dispatch to sandbox manager, handle completion/failure, apply retry strategy, respect per-thread ordering (no interleaved runs). Create src/queue/queue-manager.ts: QueueManager class - enqueue(threadId, messageId, payload), processLoop (polls for pending items across threads), respects global concurrency limit, backpressure when at capacity.

completion_spec.outcome: Queue enqueues items durably, processes FIFO per thread, retries with backoff, dead-letters after max attempts. Integration test: enqueue -> process -> complete. Failure -> retry -> dead-letter.

data.title: Channel system: connector interface, registry, router
data.description: Create src/channels/channel-types.ts: ChannelConnector interface (type, name, start, stop, onMessage, send, format), InboundEvent type, AgentOutput type (body markdown + attachments + actions), Attachment type. Create src/channels/channel-registry.ts: ChannelRegistry class - register connectors by type, get by name, start/stop all. Create src/channels/channel-router.ts: ChannelRouter class - resolvePersona(channelId, threadId) queries bindings table (specific binding first, then channel default, then drop with audit log). Create src/channels/format/markdown-converter.ts: base markdown utilities for channel-specific conversions.

completion_spec.outcome: Channel interface defined. Registry manages connectors. Router resolves persona from bindings. Unit tests for routing logic (specific binding, default, no binding).

data.title: Telegram channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/telegram/telegram-types.ts: Telegram-specific types (Update, Message, Chat). Create src/channels/connectors/telegram/telegram-format.ts: convert markdown to Telegram MarkdownV2 (escape special chars, handle code blocks, bold, italic, links). Create src/channels/connectors/telegram/telegram-connector.ts: TelegramConnector implementing ChannelConnector. Use Telegram Bot API (webhook or long-polling). Idempotency key: update_id. Normalize inbound messages to InboundEvent. Handle text, photos, documents, voice. Ingest attachments to thread attachments/ dir. Rate limiting with Telegram-specific backoff. Retry outbound sends on transient failures.

completion_spec.outcome: Telegram connector receives messages, normalizes them, and sends responses. Markdown properly converted to MarkdownV2. Unit tests for format conversion. Integration test with Telegram Bot API (mocked).

data.title: Message normalization and deduplication pipeline
data.description: Create the end-to-end message ingestion pipeline that ties channels to the queue. In the daemon's message handler: (1) Channel connector emits InboundEvent, (2) Normalize to canonical Message with idempotency_key from provider, (3) Persist to messages table (INSERT OR IGNORE for dedup), (4) If new (not duplicate): resolve thread_id (create thread if first message), resolve persona via router, enqueue queue_item, (5) If duplicate: log and skip. This is the glue code in src/daemon/ that connects channels -> messages -> threads -> bindings -> queue.

completion_spec.outcome: Inbound message flows from channel to queue item. Duplicates are silently deduplicated. New threads are created automatically. Integration test: simulate inbound message -> verify queue item created.

data.title: talond daemon: main entry point, lifecycle, signal handling
data.description: Create src/daemon/daemon.ts: TalonDaemon class - the main orchestrator. start(): load config, init DB, run migrations, init logging, start channel connectors, start queue processor, start scheduler, start sandbox manager idle reaping. stop(): drain queue, signal containers (graceful 10s), close DB, exit. Create src/daemon/signal-handler.ts: handle SIGTERM (graceful shutdown), SIGINT (same), SIGHUP (trigger hot reload). Create src/daemon/lifecycle.ts: startup sequence, shutdown sequence, crash recovery (replay durable queue, clean stale sandboxes on restart). Create src/daemon/health-check.ts: HealthCheck class - systemd watchdog ping, health status (queue depth, active containers, uptime). Create src/index.ts: entry point that instantiates and starts TalonDaemon.

completion_spec.outcome: talond starts, initializes all subsystems, handles signals, shuts down gracefully. Crash recovery replays queue on restart.

data.title: talonctl CLI: status, migrate, backup, reload, doctor
data.description: Create src/cli/index.ts: CLI entry point using commander.js. Create command modules in src/cli/commands/: status.ts (health check, active containers, queue depth - via daemon IPC or direct DB read), migrate.ts (run DB migrations), backup.ts (snapshot SQLite + data/ directory via .backup() API), reload.ts (write reload command to daemon IPC input), doctor.ts (check Docker available, check Node version, check DB writable, check data/ permissions, check config valid). setup.ts, add-channel.ts, add-persona.ts, add-skill.ts as stubs for Phase 5.

completion_spec.outcome: talonctl migrate works. talonctl status shows system state. talonctl backup creates snapshot. talonctl doctor runs checks. Unit tests for each command.

data.title: Daemon IPC: talonctl <-> talond communication
data.description: Create or extend src/ipc/daemon-ipc.ts: DaemonIpcServer (in talond) and DaemonIpcClient (in talonctl). Server polls data/ipc/daemon/input/ for command files. Commands: status (returns JSON health), reload (triggers config reload), shutdown. Client writes command files and waits for response in data/ipc/daemon/output/. Processed commands deleted from input/. Failed commands moved to errors/. Timeout handling (talonctl waits max 5s for response).

completion_spec.outcome: talonctl can communicate with running talond via file IPC. Status queries work. Reload signals work. Timeout when daemon not running.

data.title: Scheduler: cron, interval, one-shot, event-triggered
data.description: Create src/scheduler/schedule-types.ts: ScheduleType enum, ScheduleConfig. Create src/scheduler/cron-evaluator.ts: evaluate cron expressions using cron-parser, compute next run time for interval and one-shot types. Create src/scheduler/scheduler.ts: Scheduler class - tick() runs every configurable interval (default 5s), queries schedules table for due jobs (WHERE enabled=1 AND next_run_at <= now), claims with DB update (prevents double-execution on crash restart), enqueues work through queue system (same thread/persona routing), updates next_run_at after execution. Handle one-shot (disable after first run), interval (add interval to last_run_at), cron (compute next from expression), event (skip in tick, triggered externally).

completion_spec.outcome: Cron schedules fire within one tick of due time. Intervals repeat correctly. One-shots fire once and disable. No double-execution on concurrent ticks. Unit tests for cron evaluation. Integration test for schedule -> queue -> processing.

data.title: Hot reload and systemd watchdog integration
data.description: Extend src/daemon/daemon.ts with reload(): re-read config, diff against current, apply changes (add/remove channels, update personas, update policies). Active containers not affected; changes apply to new runs. For container image changes, log a warning (manual rolling restart needed). Extend src/daemon/signal-handler.ts: SIGHUP triggers reload(). Extend src/daemon/health-check.ts: systemd watchdog integration using sd_notify (or simple watchdog file touch). Create deploy/talond.service systemd unit file with WatchdogSec, Restart=on-failure, After=docker.service.

completion_spec.outcome: SIGHUP triggers config reload without restart. systemd watchdog detects hung daemon. Service file installs and starts correctly.

data.title: WhatsApp channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/whatsapp/: whatsapp-types.ts, whatsapp-format.ts (markdown to WhatsApp-flavored: bold, italic, monospace, lists only), whatsapp-connector.ts implementing ChannelConnector. Use WhatsApp Business API (Cloud API). Idempotency key: message_id. Handle text, image, document, audio message types. Ingest media to attachments/. Rate limiting per WhatsApp API limits. Retry outbound with WhatsApp-specific backoff.

completion_spec.outcome: WhatsApp connector sends and receives messages. Markdown converted to WhatsApp format. Unit tests for format conversion.

data.title: Slack channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/slack/: slack-types.ts, slack-format.ts (markdown to Slack mrkdwn dialect), slack-connector.ts implementing ChannelConnector. Use Slack Web API + Events API (or Socket Mode). Idempotency key: event_id. Thread mapping: Slack channel + thread_ts = Talon thread. Handle text, files, reactions. Rate limiting per Slack tier. Retry with Slack retry-after headers.

completion_spec.outcome: Slack connector sends and receives messages. Markdown converted to mrkdwn. Thread mapping works correctly.

data.title: Email channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/email/: email-types.ts, email-format.ts (markdown to HTML via a markdown-to-HTML library), email-connector.ts implementing ChannelConnector. Support IMAP polling for inbound and SMTP for outbound (or Gmail API as alternative). Idempotency key: Message-ID header. Thread mapping: email thread via In-Reply-To/References headers. Handle text body, HTML body, attachments. Ingest attachments to thread directory.

completion_spec.outcome: Email connector polls for inbound, sends outbound HTML. Thread tracking via email headers. Attachments ingested.

data.title: talond - Autonomous Agent Daemon v1
Project scaffolding: package.json, tsconfig, ESLint, Prettier, Vitest
data.description: Create the project skeleton. package.json with all runtime and dev dependencies (better-sqlite3, pino, neverthrow, zod, dockerode, write-file-atomic, js-yaml, cron-parser, commander, uuid; dev: typescript, vitest, @vitest/coverage-v8, eslint, prettier, tsx, @types/*). tsconfig.json with strict mode, ES2022 target, Node16 module, path aliases (@talon/*). tsconfig.build.json extending base. .eslintrc.cjs with TS strict rules + prettier. .prettierrc (2-space, single quotes, trailing commas). vitest.config.ts with 80% coverage thresholds. .gitignore (node_modules, dist, data/, coverage/). Create the full src/ directory skeleton with empty index.ts files for each module. Create tests/ directory structure mirroring src/.

completion_spec.outcome: npm install succeeds, npm run build succeeds (empty project compiles), npm test runs (no tests yet)

data.title: Core types, error types, and Result helpers
data.description: Create src/core/types/common.ts with shared types (UUID branded type, Timestamp, etc.). Create src/core/errors/error-types.ts with domain error definitions (DbError, ConfigError, IpcError, SandboxError, ToolError, ChannelError, QueueError, ScheduleError, MigrationError). Create src/core/errors/error-codes.ts with error code constants. Create src/core/types/result.ts re-exporting neverthrow types + helper functions (okVoid, errFromError, etc.). All errors should extend a base TalonError class with code, message, and optional cause.

completion_spec.outcome: Types and error definitions compile. Unit tests verify error creation and Result helper behavior.

data.title: Configuration system: Zod schemas, YAML loader, typed config
data.description: Create src/core/config/config-schema.ts with complete Zod schemas for talond.yaml: StorageConfig, SandboxConfig, ChannelConfig, PersonaConfig (with CapabilitiesSchema and MountConfigSchema), ScheduleConfig, IpcConfig, QueueConfig, SchedulerConfig, and root TalondConfigSchema. All schemas must have sensible defaults per the spec appendix. Create src/core/config/config-loader.ts: loadConfig(path) reads YAML, validates with Zod, returns Result<TalondConfig, ConfigError>. Freeze the config object (deep freeze). Create src/core/config/config-types.ts exporting z.infer types. Create config/talond.example.yaml with documented defaults.

completion_spec.outcome: Config loads from YAML, validates, returns typed config. Invalid config returns clear error messages. Unit tests for valid/invalid configs.

data.title: Logging and audit system: pino setup, audit logger
data.description: Create src/core/logging/logger.ts: createLogger(config) returns pino instance configured for structured JSON. Child logger factory: createChildLogger(parent, bindings) for adding run_id, thread_id, persona, tool, request_id. Log levels: trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal. Pretty-print in dev mode, JSON in production. Create src/core/logging/audit-logger.ts: AuditLogger class that dual-writes to both pino log and audit_log DB table. Methods: logToolExecution, logApprovalDecision, logChannelSend, logScheduleTrigger, logConfigReload. Audit entries are append-only (never delete/update).

completion_spec.outcome: Logger produces structured JSON with correlation fields. Audit logger writes to both log and DB. Unit tests for log output format and audit entry creation.

data.title: Data layer: SQLite connection, migrations, all repositories
data.description: Create src/core/database/connection.ts: createDatabase(config) opens better-sqlite3 connection with WAL mode, foreign keys ON, busy_timeout 5000. Create src/core/database/migrations/001-initial-schema.sql with full schema (12 tables: channels, personas, bindings, threads, messages, queue_items, runs, schedules, memory_items, artifacts, audit_log, tool_results) exactly as specified in plan.md section 2.1. Create src/core/database/migrations/runner.ts: runMigrations using PRAGMA user_version tracking, each migration in a transaction. Create BaseRepository class. Create all 12 repository classes with prepared statements: ChannelRepository, PersonaRepository, BindingRepository, ThreadRepository, MessageRepository (with INSERT OR IGNORE dedup), QueueRepository (with claim/complete/fail/deadLetter), RunRepository, ScheduleRepository, MemoryRepository, ArtifactRepository, AuditRepository (append-only, no update/delete), ToolResultRepository (keyed by run_id+request_id for idempotency).

completion_spec.outcome: talonctl migrate creates all tables. All repositories have insert/find/update methods with prepared statements. Unit tests for each repository. Integration test: migrate + CRUD on each table.

data.title: talonctl CLI: status, migrate, backup, reload, doctor
data.description: Create src/cli/index.ts: CLI entry point using commander.js. Create command modules in src/cli/commands/: status.ts (health check, active containers, queue depth - via daemon IPC or direct DB read), migrate.ts (run DB migrations), backup.ts (snapshot SQLite + data/ directory via .backup() API), reload.ts (write reload command to daemon IPC input), doctor.ts (check Docker available, check Node version, check DB writable, check data/ permissions, check config valid). setup.ts, add-channel.ts, add-persona.ts, add-skill.ts as stubs for Phase 5.

completion_spec.outcome: talonctl migrate works. talonctl status shows system state. talonctl backup creates snapshot. talonctl doctor runs checks. Unit tests for each command.

data.title: Daemon IPC: talonctl <-> talond communication
data.description: Create or extend src/ipc/daemon-ipc.ts: DaemonIpcServer (in talond) and DaemonIpcClient (in talonctl). Server polls data/ipc/daemon/input/ for command files. Commands: status (returns JSON health), reload (triggers config reload), shutdown. Client writes command files and waits for response in data/ipc/daemon/output/. Processed commands deleted from input/. Failed commands moved to errors/. Timeout handling (talonctl waits max 5s for response).

completion_spec.outcome: talonctl can communicate with running talond via file IPC. Status queries work. Reload signals work. Timeout when daemon not running.

data.title: Scheduler: cron, interval, one-shot, event-triggered
data.description: Create src/scheduler/schedule-types.ts: ScheduleType enum, ScheduleConfig. Create src/scheduler/cron-evaluator.ts: evaluate cron expressions using cron-parser, compute next run time for interval and one-shot types. Create src/scheduler/scheduler.ts: Scheduler class - tick() runs every configurable interval (default 5s), queries schedules table for due jobs (WHERE enabled=1 AND next_run_at <= now), claims with DB update (prevents double-execution on crash restart), enqueues work through queue system (same thread/persona routing), updates next_run_at after execution. Handle one-shot (disable after first run), interval (add interval to last_run_at), cron (compute next from expression), event (skip in tick, triggered externally).

completion_spec.outcome: Cron schedules fire within one tick of due time. Intervals repeat correctly. One-shots fire once and disable. No double-execution on concurrent ticks. Unit tests for cron evaluation. Integration test for schedule -> queue -> processing.

data.title: Hot reload and systemd watchdog integration
data.description: Extend src/daemon/daemon.ts with reload(): re-read config, diff against current, apply changes (add/remove channels, update personas, update policies). Active containers not affected; changes apply to new runs. For container image changes, log a warning (manual rolling restart needed). Extend src/daemon/signal-handler.ts: SIGHUP triggers reload(). Extend src/daemon/health-check.ts: systemd watchdog integration using sd_notify (or simple watchdog file touch). Create deploy/talond.service systemd unit file with WatchdogSec, Restart=on-failure, After=docker.service.

completion_spec.outcome: SIGHUP triggers config reload without restart. systemd watchdog detects hung daemon. Service file installs and starts correctly.

data.title: WhatsApp channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/whatsapp/: whatsapp-types.ts, whatsapp-format.ts (markdown to WhatsApp-flavored: bold, italic, monospace, lists only), whatsapp-connector.ts implementing ChannelConnector. Use WhatsApp Business API (Cloud API). Idempotency key: message_id. Handle text, image, document, audio message types. Ingest media to attachments/. Rate limiting per WhatsApp API limits. Retry outbound with WhatsApp-specific backoff.

completion_spec.outcome: WhatsApp connector sends and receives messages. Markdown converted to WhatsApp format. Unit tests for format conversion.

data.title: Slack channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/slack/: slack-types.ts, slack-format.ts (markdown to Slack mrkdwn dialect), slack-connector.ts implementing ChannelConnector. Use Slack Web API + Events API (or Socket Mode). Idempotency key: event_id. Thread mapping: Slack channel + thread_ts = Talon thread. Handle text, files, reactions. Rate limiting per Slack tier. Retry with Slack retry-after headers.

completion_spec.outcome: Slack connector sends and receives messages. Markdown converted to mrkdwn. Thread mapping works correctly.

data.title: Email channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/email/: email-types.ts, email-format.ts (markdown to HTML via a markdown-to-HTML library), email-connector.ts implementing ChannelConnector. Support IMAP polling for inbound and SMTP for outbound (or Gmail API as alternative). Idempotency key: Message-ID header. Thread mapping: email thread via In-Reply-To/References headers. Handle text body, HTML body, attachments. Ingest attachments to thread directory.

completion_spec.outcome: Email connector polls for inbound, sends outbound HTML. Thread tracking via email headers. Attachments ingested.

data.title: Discord channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/discord/: discord-types.ts, discord-format.ts (markdown to Discord-flavored - close to standard, handle code blocks, embeds), discord-connector.ts implementing ChannelConnector. Use Discord.js or raw Discord API. Idempotency key: message_id. Handle text, embeds, attachments. Rate limiting per Discord API limits.

completion_spec.outcome: Discord connector sends and receives messages. Discord-flavored markdown works. Attachments handled.

data.title: talond - Autonomous Agent Daemon v1
Project scaffolding: package.json, tsconfig, ESLint, Prettier, Vitest
data.description: Create the project skeleton. package.json with all runtime and dev dependencies (better-sqlite3, pino, neverthrow, zod, dockerode, write-file-atomic, js-yaml, cron-parser, commander, uuid; dev: typescript, vitest, @vitest/coverage-v8, eslint, prettier, tsx, @types/*). tsconfig.json with strict mode, ES2022 target, Node16 module, path aliases (@talon/*). tsconfig.build.json extending base. .eslintrc.cjs with TS strict rules + prettier. .prettierrc (2-space, single quotes, trailing commas). vitest.config.ts with 80% coverage thresholds. .gitignore (node_modules, dist, data/, coverage/). Create the full src/ directory skeleton with empty index.ts files for each module. Create tests/ directory structure mirroring src/.

completion_spec.outcome: npm install succeeds, npm run build succeeds (empty project compiles), npm test runs (no tests yet)

data.title: Core types, error types, and Result helpers
data.description: Create src/core/types/common.ts with shared types (UUID branded type, Timestamp, etc.). Create src/core/errors/error-types.ts with domain error definitions (DbError, ConfigError, IpcError, SandboxError, ToolError, ChannelError, QueueError, ScheduleError, MigrationError). Create src/core/errors/error-codes.ts with error code constants. Create src/core/types/result.ts re-exporting neverthrow types + helper functions (okVoid, errFromError, etc.). All errors should extend a base TalonError class with code, message, and optional cause.

completion_spec.outcome: Types and error definitions compile. Unit tests verify error creation and Result helper behavior.

data.title: Configuration system: Zod schemas, YAML loader, typed config
data.description: Create src/core/config/config-schema.ts with complete Zod schemas for talond.yaml: StorageConfig, SandboxConfig, ChannelConfig, PersonaConfig (with CapabilitiesSchema and MountConfigSchema), ScheduleConfig, IpcConfig, QueueConfig, SchedulerConfig, and root TalondConfigSchema. All schemas must have sensible defaults per the spec appendix. Create src/core/config/config-loader.ts: loadConfig(path) reads YAML, validates with Zod, returns Result<TalondConfig, ConfigError>. Freeze the config object (deep freeze). Create src/core/config/config-types.ts exporting z.infer types. Create config/talond.example.yaml with documented defaults.

completion_spec.outcome: Config loads from YAML, validates, returns typed config. Invalid config returns clear error messages. Unit tests for valid/invalid configs.

data.title: Logging and audit system: pino setup, audit logger
data.description: Create src/core/logging/logger.ts: createLogger(config) returns pino instance configured for structured JSON. Child logger factory: createChildLogger(parent, bindings) for adding run_id, thread_id, persona, tool, request_id. Log levels: trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal. Pretty-print in dev mode, JSON in production. Create src/core/logging/audit-logger.ts: AuditLogger class that dual-writes to both pino log and audit_log DB table. Methods: logToolExecution, logApprovalDecision, logChannelSend, logScheduleTrigger, logConfigReload. Audit entries are append-only (never delete/update).

completion_spec.outcome: Logger produces structured JSON with correlation fields. Audit logger writes to both log and DB. Unit tests for log output format and audit entry creation.

data.title: Data layer: SQLite connection, migrations, all repositories
data.description: Create src/core/database/connection.ts: createDatabase(config) opens better-sqlite3 connection with WAL mode, foreign keys ON, busy_timeout 5000. Create src/core/database/migrations/001-initial-schema.sql with full schema (12 tables: channels, personas, bindings, threads, messages, queue_items, runs, schedules, memory_items, artifacts, audit_log, tool_results) exactly as specified in plan.md section 2.1. Create src/core/database/migrations/runner.ts: runMigrations using PRAGMA user_version tracking, each migration in a transaction. Create BaseRepository class. Create all 12 repository classes with prepared statements: ChannelRepository, PersonaRepository, BindingRepository, ThreadRepository, MessageRepository (with INSERT OR IGNORE dedup), QueueRepository (with claim/complete/fail/deadLetter), RunRepository, ScheduleRepository, MemoryRepository, ArtifactRepository, AuditRepository (append-only, no update/delete), ToolResultRepository (keyed by run_id+request_id for idempotency).

completion_spec.outcome: talonctl migrate creates all tables. All repositories have insert/find/update methods with prepared statements. Unit tests for each repository. Integration test: migrate + CRUD on each table.

data.title: IPC system: atomic writer, directory poller, message types
data.description: Create src/ipc/ipc-types.ts: Zod schemas for all IPC message types (message.new, message.send, tool.request, tool.result, memory.read, memory.write, artifact.put, shutdown). Base IpcMessage schema with id (UUID), type, runId, threadId, payload, timestamp. Create src/ipc/ipc-writer.ts: atomicWrite(dir, message) using write-file-atomic (temp file + rename). File naming: {timestamp}-{id}.json. Create src/ipc/ipc-reader.ts: DirectoryPoller class - polls directory at configurable interval (default 500ms), reads files sorted by name (timestamp order), validates with Zod, emits parsed messages, deletes processed files, moves invalid files to errors/ subdirectory. Create src/ipc/ipc-channel.ts: BidirectionalIpcChannel combining writer+reader for a thread. Create src/ipc/daemon-ipc.ts: DaemonIpc for talonctl<->talond communication (same pattern, different directory).

completion_spec.outcome: IPC round-trip works: write message to input/, reader picks it up, validates, processes, deletes. Invalid messages go to errors/. Integration test with actual filesystem.

data.title: Sandbox manager: Docker container lifecycle
data.description: Create src/sandbox/sandbox-types.ts: SandboxState enum (spawning, warm, busy, shutting_down), SandboxConfig, ContainerInfo. Create src/sandbox/container-factory.ts: createContainer(config, threadId, persona) using dockerode - creates Docker container with security hardening: --cap-drop=ALL, --read-only rootfs, tmpfs /tmp, no Docker socket, resource limits (CPU, memory, PIDs), network off by default, seccomp profile. Mount thread directories (memory/ RO, artifacts/ RW, ipc/ RW). Create src/sandbox/sandbox-manager.ts: SandboxManager class - spawnContainer, getContainer(threadId), listContainers, killContainer, handleIdleTimeout (default 30min). Tracks container state, respects maxConcurrent global limit, evicts oldest-idle when at capacity. Create src/sandbox/container-health.ts: periodic health checks on warm containers.

completion_spec.outcome: Can spawn, track, and kill Docker containers programmatically. Containers are security-hardened. Integration test: spawn container, verify mounts, verify capabilities dropped, kill container.

data.title: SDK process spawner: run Claude Code inside containers
data.description: Create src/sandbox/sdk-process-spawner.ts: spawnSdkProcess(container, config) that executes Claude Code CLI inside a running Docker container using dockerode exec. Deliver initial config (secrets, persona system prompt, session ID) via stdin JSON at spawn time. Handle stdout/stderr streaming from the exec. Parse output using sentinel markers (---TALOND_OUTPUT_START--- / ---TALOND_OUTPUT_END---). Strip internal tags before passing output back. Create src/sandbox/session-tracker.ts: SessionTracker class - track SDK session IDs per thread for session resumption across messages. Handle session resumption via resume parameter when delivering follow-up messages.

completion_spec.outcome: Can execute Claude Code CLI inside a Docker container, deliver config via stdin, and parse structured output from sentinel markers. Session IDs tracked per thread.

data.title: Tool system: registry, capability resolver, policy engine
data.description: Create src/tools/tool-types.ts: ToolManifest (name, description, capabilities required, parameter schema, execution location: host/sandbox/mcp). Create src/tools/tool-registry.ts: ToolRegistry class - register tools, lookup by name, list by capability. Create src/tools/capability-resolver.ts: resolveCapabilities(persona, skill) computes intersection of persona.allow and skill.requiredCapabilities. Validate capability labels at config load time (catch typos). Create src/tools/policy-engine.ts: PolicyEngine class - evaluateToolCall(persona, tool, args) returns Allow/Deny/RequireApproval. Implemented as SDK PreToolUse hooks. Rate limiting per tool/persona. Create src/tools/approval-gate.ts: ApprovalGate placeholder (in-channel approval prompting - full implementation in Phase 6). Create src/tools/host-tools/ directory with stubs for: channel-send.ts, schedule-manage.ts, memory-access.ts, http-proxy.ts, db-query.ts.

completion_spec.outcome: Tool registry accepts manifests, capability resolver computes intersections, policy engine evaluates allow/deny/approve. Unit tests for capability resolution and policy evaluation.

data.title: Persona system: loading, capability resolution, skill integration
data.description: Create src/personas/persona-types.ts: PersonaConfig type, ResolvedPersona (with computed capabilities). Create src/personas/capability-types.ts: CapabilityLabel type (string pattern like 'fs.read:workspace'), capability validation. Create src/personas/persona-loader.ts: PersonaLoader class - loadPersonas(config) reads persona configs, resolves capabilities against skills, validates system prompt files exist, returns ResolvedPersona[]. Logs warnings for skills with unmet capabilities (not silent failure). Handles persona reload for hot-reload support.

completion_spec.outcome: Personas load from config, capabilities resolve correctly, skill integration works. Unit tests for capability resolution including edge cases (no skills, conflicting capabilities).

data.title: Memory system: thread workspace, context builder
data.description: Create src/memory/memory-types.ts: MemoryLayer enum, MemoryItem types. Create src/memory/thread-workspace.ts: ThreadWorkspace class - ensureDirectories(threadId) creates data/threads/{id}/memory/, attachments/, artifacts/, ipc/input/, ipc/output/, ipc/errors/. Manages thread directory lifecycle. Create src/memory/memory-manager.ts: MemoryManager class - readThreadMemory (from DB + files), writeMemoryItem (policy-gated by persona capabilities), getWorkingMemory (recent message window). Create src/memory/context-builder.ts: buildContext(threadId, persona) assembles context from memory layers (transcript from DB, working memory window, thread notebook files, structured facts) for agent prompts.

completion_spec.outcome: Thread directories created correctly. Memory items read/written through manager. Context builder assembles multi-layer context. Unit tests for each layer.

data.title: Queue system: durable queue, retry/backoff, dead-letter
data.description: Create src/queue/queue-types.ts: QueueItemStatus enum (pending, claimed, processing, completed, failed, dead_letter), QueueItem type. Create src/queue/retry-strategy.ts: calculateBackoff(attempt, baseMs, maxMs) with exponential backoff + jitter. Create src/queue/dead-letter.ts: DeadLetterHandler - move items to dead_letter status with human-readable reason. Create src/queue/queue-processor.ts: QueueProcessor class - dequeue next item for a thread (FIFO), claim with DB update, dispatch to sandbox manager, handle completion/failure, apply retry strategy, respect per-thread ordering (no interleaved runs). Create src/queue/queue-manager.ts: QueueManager class - enqueue(threadId, messageId, payload), processLoop (polls for pending items across threads), respects global concurrency limit, backpressure when at capacity.

completion_spec.outcome: Queue enqueues items durably, processes FIFO per thread, retries with backoff, dead-letters after max attempts. Integration test: enqueue -> process -> complete. Failure -> retry -> dead-letter.

data.title: Channel system: connector interface, registry, router
data.description: Create src/channels/channel-types.ts: ChannelConnector interface (type, name, start, stop, onMessage, send, format), InboundEvent type, AgentOutput type (body markdown + attachments + actions), Attachment type. Create src/channels/channel-registry.ts: ChannelRegistry class - register connectors by type, get by name, start/stop all. Create src/channels/channel-router.ts: ChannelRouter class - resolvePersona(channelId, threadId) queries bindings table (specific binding first, then channel default, then drop with audit log). Create src/channels/format/markdown-converter.ts: base markdown utilities for channel-specific conversions.

completion_spec.outcome: Channel interface defined. Registry manages connectors. Router resolves persona from bindings. Unit tests for routing logic (specific binding, default, no binding).

data.title: Telegram channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/telegram/telegram-types.ts: Telegram-specific types (Update, Message, Chat). Create src/channels/connectors/telegram/telegram-format.ts: convert markdown to Telegram MarkdownV2 (escape special chars, handle code blocks, bold, italic, links). Create src/channels/connectors/telegram/telegram-connector.ts: TelegramConnector implementing ChannelConnector. Use Telegram Bot API (webhook or long-polling). Idempotency key: update_id. Normalize inbound messages to InboundEvent. Handle text, photos, documents, voice. Ingest attachments to thread attachments/ dir. Rate limiting with Telegram-specific backoff. Retry outbound sends on transient failures.

completion_spec.outcome: Telegram connector receives messages, normalizes them, and sends responses. Markdown properly converted to MarkdownV2. Unit tests for format conversion. Integration test with Telegram Bot API (mocked).

data.title: Message normalization and deduplication pipeline
data.description: Create the end-to-end message ingestion pipeline that ties channels to the queue. In the daemon's message handler: (1) Channel connector emits InboundEvent, (2) Normalize to canonical Message with idempotency_key from provider, (3) Persist to messages table (INSERT OR IGNORE for dedup), (4) If new (not duplicate): resolve thread_id (create thread if first message), resolve persona via router, enqueue queue_item, (5) If duplicate: log and skip. This is the glue code in src/daemon/ that connects channels -> messages -> threads -> bindings -> queue.

completion_spec.outcome: Inbound message flows from channel to queue item. Duplicates are silently deduplicated. New threads are created automatically. Integration test: simulate inbound message -> verify queue item created.

data.title: talond daemon: main entry point, lifecycle, signal handling
data.description: Create src/daemon/daemon.ts: TalonDaemon class - the main orchestrator. start(): load config, init DB, run migrations, init logging, start channel connectors, start queue processor, start scheduler, start sandbox manager idle reaping. stop(): drain queue, signal containers (graceful 10s), close DB, exit. Create src/daemon/signal-handler.ts: handle SIGTERM (graceful shutdown), SIGINT (same), SIGHUP (trigger hot reload). Create src/daemon/lifecycle.ts: startup sequence, shutdown sequence, crash recovery (replay durable queue, clean stale sandboxes on restart). Create src/daemon/health-check.ts: HealthCheck class - systemd watchdog ping, health status (queue depth, active containers, uptime). Create src/index.ts: entry point that instantiates and starts TalonDaemon.

completion_spec.outcome: talond starts, initializes all subsystems, handles signals, shuts down gracefully. Crash recovery replays queue on restart.

Methods: logToolExecution, logApprovalDecision, logChannelSend, logScheduleTrigger, logConfigReload. Audit entries are append-only (never delete/update).

completion_spec.outcome: Logger produces structured JSON with correlation fields. Audit logger writes to both log and DB. Unit tests for log output format and audit entry creation.

data.title: Data layer: SQLite connection, migrations, all repositories
data.description: Create src/core/database/connection.ts: createDatabase(config) opens better-sqlite3 connection with WAL mode, foreign keys ON, busy_timeout 5000. Create src/core/database/migrations/001-initial-schema.sql with full schema (12 tables: channels, personas, bindings, threads, messages, queue_items, runs, schedules, memory_items, artifacts, audit_log, tool_results) exactly as specified in plan.md section 2.1. Create src/core/database/migrations/runner.ts: runMigrations using PRAGMA user_version tracking, each migration in a transaction. Create BaseRepository class. Create all 12 repository classes with prepared statements: ChannelRepository, PersonaRepository, BindingRepository, ThreadRepository, MessageRepository (with INSERT OR IGNORE dedup), QueueRepository (with claim/complete/fail/deadLetter), RunRepository, ScheduleRepository, MemoryRepository, ArtifactRepository, AuditRepository (append-only, no update/delete), ToolResultRepository (keyed by run_id+request_id for idempotency).

completion_spec.outcome: talonctl migrate creates all tables. All repositories have insert/find/update methods with prepared statements. Unit tests for each repository. Integration test: migrate + CRUD on each table.

data.title: IPC system: atomic writer, directory poller, message types
data.description: Create src/ipc/ipc-types.ts: Zod schemas for all IPC message types (message.new, message.send, tool.request, tool.result, memory.read, memory.write, artifact.put, shutdown). Base IpcMessage schema with id (UUID), type, runId, threadId, payload, timestamp. Create src/ipc/ipc-writer.ts: atomicWrite(dir, message) using write-file-atomic (temp file + rename). File naming: {timestamp}-{id}.json. Create src/ipc/ipc-reader.ts: DirectoryPoller class - polls directory at configurable interval (default 500ms), reads files sorted by name (timestamp order), validates with Zod, emits parsed messages, deletes processed files, moves invalid files to errors/ subdirectory. Create src/ipc/ipc-channel.ts: BidirectionalIpcChannel combining writer+reader for a thread. Create src/ipc/daemon-ipc.ts: DaemonIpc for talonctl<->talond communication (same pattern, different directory).

completion_spec.outcome: IPC round-trip works: write message to input/, reader picks it up, validates, processes, deletes. Invalid messages go to errors/. Integration test with actual filesystem.

data.title: Sandbox manager: Docker container lifecycle
data.description: Create src/sandbox/sandbox-types.ts: SandboxState enum (spawning, warm, busy, shutting_down), SandboxConfig, ContainerInfo. Create src/sandbox/container-factory.ts: createContainer(config, threadId, persona) using dockerode - creates Docker container with security hardening: --cap-drop=ALL, --read-only rootfs, tmpfs /tmp, no Docker socket, resource limits (CPU, memory, PIDs), network off by default, seccomp profile. Mount thread directories (memory/ RO, artifacts/ RW, ipc/ RW). Create src/sandbox/sandbox-manager.ts: SandboxManager class - spawnContainer, getContainer(threadId), listContainers, killContainer, handleIdleTimeout (default 30min). Tracks container state, respects maxConcurrent global limit, evicts oldest-idle when at capacity. Create src/sandbox/container-health.ts: periodic health checks on warm containers.

completion_spec.outcome: Can spawn, track, and kill Docker containers programmatically. Containers are security-hardened. Integration test: spawn container, verify mounts, verify capabilities dropped, kill container.

data.title: SDK process spawner: run Claude Code inside containers
data.description: Create src/sandbox/sdk-process-spawner.ts: spawnSdkProcess(container, config) that executes Claude Code CLI inside a running Docker container using dockerode exec. Deliver initial config (secrets, persona system prompt, session ID) via stdin JSON at spawn time. Handle stdout/stderr streaming from the exec. Parse output using sentinel markers (---TALOND_OUTPUT_START--- / ---TALOND_OUTPUT_END---). Strip internal tags before passing output back. Create src/sandbox/session-tracker.ts: SessionTracker class - track SDK session IDs per thread for session resumption across messages. Handle session resumption via resume parameter when delivering follow-up messages.

completion_spec.outcome: Can execute Claude Code CLI inside a Docker container, deliver config via stdin, and parse structured output from sentinel markers. Session IDs tracked per thread.

data.title: Tool system: registry, capability resolver, policy engine
data.description: Create src/tools/tool-types.ts: ToolManifest (name, description, capabilities required, parameter schema, execution location: host/sandbox/mcp). Create src/tools/tool-registry.ts: ToolRegistry class - register tools, lookup by name, list by capability. Create src/tools/capability-resolver.ts: resolveCapabilities(persona, skill) computes intersection of persona.allow and skill.requiredCapabilities. Validate capability labels at config load time (catch typos). Create src/tools/policy-engine.ts: PolicyEngine class - evaluateToolCall(persona, tool, args) returns Allow/Deny/RequireApproval. Implemented as SDK PreToolUse hooks. Rate limiting per tool/persona. Create src/tools/approval-gate.ts: ApprovalGate placeholder (in-channel approval prompting - full implementation in Phase 6). Create src/tools/host-tools/ directory with stubs for: channel-send.ts, schedule-manage.ts, memory-access.ts, http-proxy.ts, db-query.ts.

completion_spec.outcome: Tool registry accepts manifests, capability resolver computes intersections, policy engine evaluates allow/deny/approve. Unit tests for capability resolution and policy evaluation.

data.title: Persona system: loading, capability resolution, skill integration
data.description: Create src/personas/persona-types.ts: PersonaConfig type, ResolvedPersona (with computed capabilities). Create src/personas/capability-types.ts: CapabilityLabel type (string pattern like 'fs.read:workspace'), capability validation. Create src/personas/persona-loader.ts: PersonaLoader class - loadPersonas(config) reads persona configs, resolves capabilities against skills, validates system prompt files exist, returns ResolvedPersona[]. Logs warnings for skills with unmet capabilities (not silent failure). Handles persona reload for hot-reload support.

completion_spec.outcome: Personas load from config, capabilities resolve correctly, skill integration works. Unit tests for capability resolution including edge cases (no skills, conflicting capabilities).

data.title: Memory system: thread workspace, context builder
data.description: Create src/memory/memory-types.ts: MemoryLayer enum, MemoryItem types. Create src/memory/thread-workspace.ts: ThreadWorkspace class - ensureDirectories(threadId) creates data/threads/{id}/memory/, attachments/, artifacts/, ipc/input/, ipc/output/, ipc/errors/. Manages thread directory lifecycle. Create src/memory/memory-manager.ts: MemoryManager class - readThreadMemory (from DB + files), writeMemoryItem (policy-gated by persona capabilities), getWorkingMemory (recent message window). Create src/memory/context-builder.ts: buildContext(threadId, persona) assembles context from memory layers (transcript from DB, working memory window, thread notebook files, structured facts) for agent prompts.

completion_spec.outcome: Thread directories created correctly. Memory items read/written through manager. Context builder assembles multi-layer context. Unit tests for each layer.

data.title: Queue system: durable queue, retry/backoff, dead-letter
data.description: Create src/queue/queue-types.ts: QueueItemStatus enum (pending, claimed, processing, completed, failed, dead_letter), QueueItem type. Create src/queue/retry-strategy.ts: calculateBackoff(attempt, baseMs, maxMs) with exponential backoff + jitter. Create src/queue/dead-letter.ts: DeadLetterHandler - move items to dead_letter status with human-readable reason. Create src/queue/queue-processor.ts: QueueProcessor class - dequeue next item for a thread (FIFO), claim with DB update, dispatch to sandbox manager, handle completion/failure, apply retry strategy, respect per-thread ordering (no interleaved runs). Create src/queue/queue-manager.ts: QueueManager class - enqueue(threadId, messageId, payload), processLoop (polls for pending items across threads), respects global concurrency limit, backpressure when at capacity.

completion_spec.outcome: Queue enqueues items durably, processes FIFO per thread, retries with backoff, dead-letters after max attempts. Integration test: enqueue -> process -> complete. Failure -> retry -> dead-letter.

data.title: Channel system: connector interface, registry, router
data.description: Create src/channels/channel-types.ts: ChannelConnector interface (type, name, start, stop, onMessage, send, format), InboundEvent type, AgentOutput type (body markdown + attachments + actions), Attachment type. Create src/channels/channel-registry.ts: ChannelRegistry class - register connectors by type, get by name, start/stop all. Create src/channels/channel-router.ts: ChannelRouter class - resolvePersona(channelId, threadId) queries bindings table (specific binding first, then channel default, then drop with audit log). Create src/channels/format/markdown-converter.ts: base markdown utilities for channel-specific conversions.

completion_spec.outcome: Channel interface defined. Registry manages connectors. Router resolves persona from bindings. Unit tests for routing logic (specific binding, default, no binding).

data.title: Telegram channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/telegram/telegram-types.ts: Telegram-specific types (Update, Message, Chat). Create src/channels/connectors/telegram/telegram-format.ts: convert markdown to Telegram MarkdownV2 (escape special chars, handle code blocks, bold, italic, links). Create src/channels/connectors/telegram/telegram-connector.ts: TelegramConnector implementing ChannelConnector. Use Telegram Bot API (webhook or long-polling). Idempotency key: update_id. Normalize inbound messages to InboundEvent. Handle text, photos, documents, voice. Ingest attachments to thread attachments/ dir. Rate limiting with Telegram-specific backoff. Retry outbound sends on transient failures.

completion_spec.outcome: Telegram connector receives messages, normalizes them, and sends responses. Markdown properly converted to MarkdownV2. Unit tests for format conversion. Integration test with Telegram Bot API (mocked).

data.title: Message normalization and deduplication pipeline
data.description: Create the end-to-end message ingestion pipeline that ties channels to the queue. In the daemon's message handler: (1) Channel connector emits InboundEvent, (2) Normalize to canonical Message with idempotency_key from provider, (3) Persist to messages table (INSERT OR IGNORE for dedup), (4) If new (not duplicate): resolve thread_id (create thread if first message), resolve persona via router, enqueue queue_item, (5) If duplicate: log and skip. This is the glue code in src/daemon/ that connects channels -> messages -> threads -> bindings -> queue.

completion_spec.outcome: Inbound message flows from channel to queue item. Duplicates are silently deduplicated. New threads are created automatically. Integration test: simulate inbound message -> verify queue item created.

data.title: talond daemon: main entry point, lifecycle, signal handling
data.description: Create src/daemon/daemon.ts: TalonDaemon class - the main orchestrator. start(): load config, init DB, run migrations, init logging, start channel connectors, start queue processor, start scheduler, start sandbox manager idle reaping. stop(): drain queue, signal containers (graceful 10s), close DB, exit. Create src/daemon/signal-handler.ts: handle SIGTERM (graceful shutdown), SIGINT (same), SIGHUP (trigger hot reload). Create src/daemon/lifecycle.ts: startup sequence, shutdown sequence, crash recovery (replay durable queue, clean stale sandboxes on restart). Create src/daemon/health-check.ts: HealthCheck class - systemd watchdog ping, health status (queue depth, active containers, uptime). Create src/index.ts: entry point that instantiates and starts TalonDaemon.

completion_spec.outcome: talond starts, initializes all subsystems, handles signals, shuts down gracefully. Crash recovery replays queue on restart.

data.title: talonctl CLI: status, migrate, backup, reload, doctor
data.description: Create src/cli/index.ts: CLI entry point using commander.js. Create command modules in src/cli/commands/: status.ts (health check, active containers, queue depth - via daemon IPC or direct DB read), migrate.ts (run DB migrations), backup.ts (snapshot SQLite + data/ directory via .backup() API), reload.ts (write reload command to daemon IPC input), doctor.ts (check Docker available, check Node version, check DB writable, check data/ permissions, check config valid). setup.ts, add-channel.ts, add-persona.ts, add-skill.ts as stubs for Phase 5.

completion_spec.outcome: talonctl migrate works. talonctl status shows system state. talonctl backup creates snapshot. talonctl doctor runs checks. Unit tests for each command.

data.title: Daemon IPC: talonctl <-> talond communication
data.description: Create or extend src/ipc/daemon-ipc.ts: DaemonIpcServer (in talond) and DaemonIpcClient (in talonctl). Server polls data/ipc/daemon/input/ for command files. Commands: status (returns JSON health), reload (triggers config reload), shutdown. Client writes command files and waits for response in data/ipc/daemon/output/. Processed commands deleted from input/. Failed commands moved to errors/. Timeout handling (talonctl waits max 5s for response).

completion_spec.outcome: talonctl can communicate with running talond via file IPC. Status queries work. Reload signals work. Timeout when daemon not running.

data.title: Scheduler: cron, interval, one-shot, event-triggered
data.description: Create src/scheduler/schedule-types.ts: ScheduleType enum, ScheduleConfig. Create src/scheduler/cron-evaluator.ts: evaluate cron expressions using cron-parser, compute next run time for interval and one-shot types. Create src/scheduler/scheduler.ts: Scheduler class - tick() runs every configurable interval (default 5s), queries schedules table for due jobs (WHERE enabled=1 AND next_run_at <= now), claims with DB update (prevents double-execution on crash restart), enqueues work through queue system (same thread/persona routing), updates next_run_at after execution. Handle one-shot (disable after first run), interval (add interval to last_run_at), cron (compute next from expression), event (skip in tick, triggered externally).

completion_spec.outcome: Cron schedules fire within one tick of due time. Intervals repeat correctly. One-shots fire once and disable. No double-execution on concurrent ticks. Unit tests for cron evaluation. Integration test for schedule -> queue -> processing.

data.title: Hot reload and systemd watchdog integration
data.description: Extend src/daemon/daemon.ts with reload(): re-read config, diff against current, apply changes (add/remove channels, update personas, update policies). Active containers not affected; changes apply to new runs. For container image changes, log a warning (manual rolling restart needed). Extend src/daemon/signal-handler.ts: SIGHUP triggers reload(). Extend src/daemon/health-check.ts: systemd watchdog integration using sd_notify (or simple watchdog file touch). Create deploy/talond.service systemd unit file with WatchdogSec, Restart=on-failure, After=docker.service.

completion_spec.outcome: SIGHUP triggers config reload without restart. systemd watchdog detects hung daemon. Service file installs and starts correctly.

data.title: WhatsApp channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/whatsapp/: whatsapp-types.ts, whatsapp-format.ts (markdown to WhatsApp-flavored: bold, italic, monospace, lists only), whatsapp-connector.ts implementing ChannelConnector. Use WhatsApp Business API (Cloud API). Idempotency key: message_id. Handle text, image, document, audio message types. Ingest media to attachments/. Rate limiting per WhatsApp API limits. Retry outbound with WhatsApp-specific backoff.

completion_spec.outcome: WhatsApp connector sends and receives messages. Markdown converted to WhatsApp format. Unit tests for format conversion.

data.title: Slack channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/slack/: slack-types.ts, slack-format.ts (markdown to Slack mrkdwn dialect), slack-connector.ts implementing ChannelConnector. Use Slack Web API + Events API (or Socket Mode). Idempotency key: event_id. Thread mapping: Slack channel + thread_ts = Talon thread. Handle text, files, reactions. Rate limiting per Slack tier. Retry with Slack retry-after headers.

completion_spec.outcome: Slack connector sends and receives messages. Markdown converted to mrkdwn. Thread mapping works correctly.

data.title: Email channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/email/: email-types.ts, email-format.ts (markdown to HTML via a markdown-to-HTML library), email-connector.ts implementing ChannelConnector. Support IMAP polling for inbound and SMTP for outbound (or Gmail API as alternative). Idempotency key: Message-ID header. Thread mapping: email thread via In-Reply-To/References headers. Handle text body, HTML body, attachments. Ingest attachments to thread directory.

completion_spec.outcome: Email connector polls for inbound, sends outbound HTML. Thread tracking via email headers. Attachments ingested.

data.title: Discord channel connector
data.description: Create src/channels/connectors/discord/: discord-types.ts, discord-format.ts (markdown to Discord-flavored - close to standard, handle code blocks, embeds), discord-connector.ts implementing ChannelConnector. Use Discord.js or raw Discord API. Idempotency key: message_id. Handle text, embeds, attachments. Rate limiting per Discord API limits.

completion_spec.outcome: Discord connector sends and receives messages. Discord-flavored markdown works. Attachments handled.

data.title: Skills system: loader, resolver, migrations
data.description: Create src/skills/skill-types.ts: SkillManifest (name, requiredCapabilities, config schema, prompt fragments, tool manifests, MCP server defs, migrations). Create src/skills/skill-loader.ts: SkillLoader class - load skill from directory (skills/{name}/skill.yaml + prompts/*.md + tools/*.yaml + mcp/*.json + migrations/*.sql). Validate manifest. Create src/skills/skill-resolver.ts: SkillResolver class - resolve skill capabilities against persona allowlist, merge prompt fragments into persona system prompt, register skill tools in tool registry. Skill migrations run during talonctl migrate (after core migrations).

completion_spec.outcome: Skills load from directory, capabilities resolve, prompt fragments merge, tool manifests register. Warning on unmet capabilities. Unit tests for resolution logic.

data.title: MCP integration: proxy, allowlists
data.description: Create src/mcp/mcp-types.ts: McpServerConfig, McpToolCall. Create src/mcp/mcp-registry.ts: McpRegistry class - register MCP servers from config, manage lifecycle (start/stop). Create src/mcp/mcp-proxy.ts: McpProxy class - receives MCP tool calls from sandbox IPC, checks policy (persona MCP allowlist, tool allowlist, argument validation, rate limits), forwards to appropriate MCP server, returns result. Each MCP server has its own credential scope. MCP server failures do not crash talond (catch and return error to sandbox).

completion_spec.outcome: MCP servers register, tool calls proxy through policy check, failures isolated. Unit tests for policy checking. Integration test with mock MCP server.

data.title: talond - Autonomous Agent Daemon v1
Project scaffolding: package.json, tsconfig, ESLint, Prettier, Vitest
data.description: Create the project skeleton. package.json with all runtime and dev dependencies (better-sqlite3, pino, neverthrow, zod, dockerode, write-file-atomic, js-yaml, cron-parser, commander, uuid; dev: typescript, vitest, @vitest/coverage-v8, eslint, prettier, tsx, @types/*). tsconfig.json with strict mode, ES2022 target, Node16 module, path aliases (@talon/*). tsconfig.build.json extending base. .eslintrc.cjs with TS strict rules + prettier. .prettierrc (2-space, single quotes, trailing commas). vitest.config.ts with 80% coverage thresholds. .gitignore (node_modules, dist, data/, coverage/). Create the full src/ directory skeleton with empty index.ts files for each module. Create tests/ directory structure mirroring src/.

completion_spec.outcome: npm install succeeds, npm run build succeeds (empty project compiles), npm test runs (no tests yet)

data.title: Core types, error types, and Result helpers
data.description: Create src/core/types/common.ts with shared types (UUID branded type, Timestamp, etc.). Create src/core/errors/error-types.ts with domain error definitions (DbError, ConfigError, IpcError, SandboxError, ToolError, ChannelError, QueueError, ScheduleError, MigrationError). Create src/core/errors/error-codes.ts with error code constants. Create src/core/types/result.ts re-exporting neverthrow types + helper functions (okVoid, errFromError, etc.). All errors should extend a base TalonError class with code, message, and optional cause.

completion_spec.outcome: Types and error definitions compile. Unit tests verify error creation and Result helper behavior.

data.title: Configuration system: Zod schemas, YAML loader, typed config
data.description: Create src/core/config/config-schema.ts with complete Zod schemas for talond.yaml: StorageConfig, SandboxConfig, ChannelConfig, PersonaConfig (with CapabilitiesSchema and MountConfigSchema), ScheduleConfig, IpcConfig, QueueConfig, SchedulerConfig, and root TalondConfigSchema. All schemas must have sensible defaults per the spec appendix. Create src/core/config/config-loader.ts: loadConfig(path) reads YAML, validates with Zod, returns Result<TalondConfig, ConfigError>. Freeze the config object (deep freeze). Create src/core/config/config-types.ts exporting z.infer types. Create config/talond.example.yaml with documented defaults.

completion_spec.outcome: Config loads from YAML, validates, returns typed config. Invalid config returns clear error messages. Unit tests for valid/invalid configs.
